ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Milo milkshake; closest parent is chocolate milk beverage (FOODON_03301737).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (chocolate-flavoured milk-based drink) used as closest parent; no exact code for Milo milkshake exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 (Chocolate milk) used as closest parent; no exact concept for Milo milkshake exists.

main_ingredients: Milo powder (malted barley, cocoa, sugar, skimmed milk powder, vitamins and minerals)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Milo Milkshake is approximately one cup (260 g / 250 ml), prepared by blending Milo powder with full-cream milk and ice.

nutrient_source.title: Milo Chocolate Malt Beverage Powder — Nestlé Product Nutritional Information (Singapore); supplemented with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for whole milk (NDB 01077) for the milk component; composite estimate for a typical 3 tablespoons Milo powder (30 g) in 230 ml whole milk preparation.
nutrient_source.organization: Nestlé Singapore / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from Nestlé Singapore Milo powder label data (per 100 g powder) and USDA SR Legacy whole milk data, combined in a typical milkshake ratio (approximately 30 g Milo powder + 230 ml whole milk per 260 g serving). Per-100 g values are back-calculated from this composite. This is a restaurant-served beverage; exact recipe may vary. Values represent a reasonable central estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; Milo beverage and chocolate milk analogous estimates; University of Sydney GI Database entry for chocolate milk (GI ~34–43 range).
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Milo milkshake as served in Singapore. GI of 40 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for chocolate milk (GI approximately 34–43, mean ~40, glucose reference). Milo milkshake shares the same primary glycemic drivers: lactose from milk and added sugars from Milo powder. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 29.9) / 100 ≈ 12.0, where carbohydrate per 260 g serving ≈ 29.9 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~55 mg per 100 g; ~143 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single serving of Milo Milkshake contributes approximately 143 mg sodium, which is modest relative to the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; however, individuals with hypertension should account for added sugar and total caloric load when consuming sweetened milk beverages regularly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar and moderate glycemic load (~12 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Milo Milkshake has a low glycemic index (~40) but delivers approximately 30 g of carbohydrate and 26 g of sugar per serving, resulting in a glycemic load of ~12; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, avoid additional sugar, and monitor postprandial glucose response when consuming this beverage.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and added sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: A serving of Milo Milkshake provides approximately 4.4 g of saturated fat and 26 g of sugar; while the saturated fat content is moderate, regular consumption of high-sugar beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides and should be limited in individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Milo Milkshake is a popular sweetened malted chocolate milk beverage widely consumed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is prepared by blending Nestlé Milo powder — a malted barley and cocoa-based drink mix fortified with vitamins and minerals — with full-cream milk and typically ice, producing a cold, creamy, chocolatey drink. The beverage has a rich, malty-sweet flavour with a smooth texture and a characteristic cocoa-malt aroma. In Singapore, it is commonly served at hawker centres, kopitiams, and Indian-Muslim eateries, often prepared to order. Nutritionally, a standard 260 g serving provides approximately 208 kcal, 9.1 g protein, 7.3 g fat, and 29.9 g carbohydrate, along with meaningful amounts of calcium (~312 mg), iron (~3.1 mg), and B-vitamins contributed by the Milo powder fortification. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low GI), though the glycemic load per serving is moderate (~12) due to the volume of carbohydrate delivered. Clinically, the beverage is of moderate concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes owing to its sugar content, and should be consumed in controlled portions. It is suitable for vegetarians and is generally considered halal. The drink is culturally significant in Singapore as a nostalgic childhood beverage and remains a staple across all age groups.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Milo milkshake?
What is the GI of Milo milkshake?
How much sugar is in a Milo milkshake?
What nutrients does Milo milkshake contain?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from Nestlé Singapore Milo powder label data and USDA SR Legacy whole milk data combined in a typical milkshake preparation ratio. No single approved primary source (HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA) provides a direct entry for a Milo milkshake as a prepared beverage. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate milk. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates; exact values will vary depending on the Milo-to-milk ratio used by the restaurant. The record is based on a standard preparation of approximately 30 g Milo powder in 230 ml whole milk (total ~260 g serving). 2. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per Nestlé Singapore Milo powder label (no partially hydrogenated oils used). 3. GI of 40 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for chocolate milk; no direct GI measurement for a Milo milkshake as a restaurant-prepared beverage was found in the approved GI source database. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g (0.8) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1) are estimated from the USDA SR Legacy whole milk fatty acid profile proportionally applied to the composite fat content. 5. The cuisine tag in the input JSON was listed as 'Indian'; this has been reclassified to 'Singaporean' as Milo milkshake is a pan-Singaporean beverage not specific to Indian cuisine, though it is commonly served at Indian-Muslim eateries. 6. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean cuisine category: Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. 7. All -1 sentinel values in per_100g have been replaced with actual estimated values where derivable from the composite source; no -1 values remain in this record.
